paragraphs.body: My name is Jake Max, and I am a student at the University of Pittsburgh. I recently read, “The Girl,” which focused on the important idea of rigid expectations placed on the you in your childhood as well as the expectations placed on girls in general by society. Throughout this work I noticed your impressive use of an unconventional structure and the power of your emotion in your writing. The unconventional structure of the essay with its run on sentences in quick succession helped me feel the emotion of the story. The overwhelming number of expectations that the speaker must meet throughout the story is quite evident due to all the commands and the use of a broken-up run on sentence. I also admired your use of breaks in the run on sentence which were always italicized and were questions. My sisters have experienced these rigid expectations at some point in their lives. I know that more was expected from my sisters simply because they were older and, they had to deal with different societal expectations for women as well. I know from my experience observing and talking about how society demands different expectations from my sisters, you had to deal with similar things. I wonder what the true significance of the questions were in the passage. I have a feeling that the questions could represent you in way. The way the questions are phrased makes think of you as innocent and a victim in a sense. I would like to thank you for your wonderful and very original and creative essay that took on pressing and important topics that I had not considered the weight of before. Your writing gave me a new perspective I have only experienced through the eyes of my siblings.

annotations.comment: You're really getting at the heart of Kincaid's writing. I would love to see you explore your own connections to the piece--for instance, you mention your sisters, and that's great, but how have societal expectations impacted you as well? When have you experienced these sorts of pressures? This is a great start, I'm looking forward to seeing what you do next!
